message:
fix(security): deterministic iteration, HMAC-SHA256, threshold sig dedup, and unwrap removal

Systematic security hardening across 8 modules:

HashMap → BTreeMap migration (5 files):
- challenge_flow, impossible_default, interface_hash, trust_complexity,
  trust_zone: Replace HashMap with BTreeMap throughout to guarantee
  deterministic iteration order. This eliminates a class of
  non-determinism bugs where security decisions could vary across runs
  depending on hash seed randomization.

HMAC fix (remote_cap.rs):
- Replace naive SHA256(secret|payload) keyed digest with proper
  HMAC-SHA256. The previous construction was vulnerable to
  length-extension attacks. Includes regression test proving the two
  constructions produce different output.

Threshold signature dedup (threshold_sig.rs):
- Add seen_key_ids deduplication set so a single signing key cannot
  contribute twice toward quorum even if presented under different
  signer IDs. Prevents a signer from inflating their weight by
  registering the same key multiple times. Includes test
  duplicate_key_with_different_signer_id_counted_once.

Unwrap removal (staking_governance.rs):
- Replace two .unwrap() calls with proper .ok_or_else() error returns,
  eliminating panic paths in the governance staking module.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
